title: Diabetic kidney screening result review

examination.text: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.
examination.rationale: The exact DHA annual-review section supports measured vital signs, weight or BMI and home-monitoring context for adult type 2 diabetes kidney screening.
examination.evidence_extract: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

investigations.text: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.
investigations.rationale: The exact KDIGO detection and chronicity section supports interval change, associated context, relevant negatives and clinician-reviewed GFR, albuminuria, prior trend or imaging documentation without diagnosing CKD from one result.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Detection of CKD and evaluation of chronicity
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 1.1.1.1–1.1.1.2 and 1.1.3.1–1.1.3.2; summary PDF pages 34 and 54–57

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact monitoring section supports clinician concern, entered plan and arranged follow-up only when decided, without generating a stage or interval.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Monitoring GFR and albuminuria in CKD
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 2.1.1–2.1.5; summary PDF pages 40 and 81–82

follow_up.text: Supports ACR, PCR, urinalysis, quantitative confirmation, and interpretation factors including haematuria, menstruation, exercise, and infection.
follow_up.rationale: The exact albuminuria section supports patient concerns, focused assessment and urine-result context only when assessed or reviewed; it does not infer chronicity or cause.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports ACR, PCR, urinalysis, quantitative confirmation, and interpretation factors including haematuria, menstruation, exercise, and infection.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Evaluation of albuminuria
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 1.3.1.1–1.3.1.3 and Table 16; summary PDF pages 39 and 75–76

sources_used.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

source_limitations: Creatinine, eGFR, ACR, urinalysis, haematuria, prior trends, acute illness, medicine, CKD stage, cause, diagnosis, treatment and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
